document.subject: TBG Contracting
document.notes: First canonical dossier in the Sage OS. TBG is an active client, not a cold prospect. This record documents the full relationship state: the Phase 1 brand deliverables Sage completed, the Phase 2 systems build in progress with The G Fraction, and the growth architecture from solo operator to $15M commercial construction firm.

prospect.public_tagline: Your Project. Your Budget. Your Builder.
prospect.shared_notes: Shared client with The G Fraction. The foundational proof-of-concept for the Sage + G Fraction partnership. Phase 1 (brand, website, GMB) delivered by Sage. Phase 2 (Cornerstone brain, Base44 app, GHL, Bland.ai) in progress with The G Fraction as lead architect.
prospect.research_summary: TBG is a solo commercial construction operation run by Matt Ecklund out of the Twin Cities, with coverage across central Minnesota. Project range spans $10K punch-list work to $10M ground-up builds — unusually wide for a single operator, and deliberately so. The business is faith-grounded internally with a universal external voice. Matt is the signal: every job runs through him personally, from quote to closeout. The growth arc is solo today, crew of five within five years, $500K this year scaling to $15M by Year 5. Relationship strength is the moat — general contractors, property managers, community ties. The brand had to reflect all of it: craft, scale, stewardship, and the personal touch of a builder who answers his own phone.
prospect.notes_rich: Matt is one of the most considered clients I've worked with. He knows what he wants, he knows what he doesn't, and he knows when to trust the people he's working with to do their job. The brand work took longer than usual because it had to hold the weight of a business that spans from a $15K punch list to a $6M office buildout without feeling like either was an afterthought. The dual-register voice — faith-grounded inside the company, universal outside — is the single hardest thing we've encoded into a brand system. We got it right. Now Geoffrey is building the system that runs on top of it.

client.monthly_retainer_structure: To be set at Cornerstone go-live. Proposed: ongoing systems retainer split between Sage (creative maintenance) and The G Fraction (systems operations).
client.one_time_total_structure: Phase 1 brand + website billed against Sage's standard Launch Package pricing, adjusted for case-study discount per methodology.
client.ltv_forecast_notes: Long-term value scales with Matt's growth trajectory. Year 1 ($500K revenue) retainer modest; by Year 5 ($15M revenue) the systems partnership is a meaningful recurring engagement. Revenue share may be revisited as Cornerstone proves out.
client.notes_rich: TBG is the case study. Every decision made here becomes the template for the next service-business we stamp the model onto: HVAC, plumbing, landscaping, remodeling. Handle accordingly.

meta.ai_generation_log: Authored as canonical first dossier, 2026-04-20. Written in Benedict Frank's voice. Manually curated rather than fully AI-generated because TBG is the reference case.

feedback.content: Ben — this is exactly it. You captured what I've been trying to say for a year. Let's run it. Green light on Phase 2, tell Geoffrey to go.

section_01_opening.headline: This is your dossier, Matt.
body: Somewhere between our first conversation and today, TBG became one of the most considered businesses I've built for. What started as a logo turned into a full brand, a website that tells the right story, and a backend system being architected by Geoffrey and The G Fraction to hold the weight of what you're building.

This page is a dossier in the old sense of the word. Part memory, part map. A single place that remembers what we've made, what's being built right now, and what comes next. It lives quietly at a private URL, only shared with you.
Read it at your own pace. Nothing here needs an answer today.

section_02_what_we_see.headline: A man's company, in the craft sense. Built to scale without losing the signal.
body: TBG is not a construction company that wants to look bigger than it is. It's a solo operator who knows exactly how to run a $15K punch-list and a $6M ground-up build with the same hand. That range is the advantage. Most commercial firms won't touch the small work. Most handymen can't hold the big. You can do both, and the brand had to hold both without embarrassing either one.

The dual register is the hardest thing to encode and the most valuable thing you have. Faith-grounded inside — the values, the stewardship, the way you show up — and universal outside, so every client, whether they share your faith or not, feels the same care. We spent more time on voice than on anything else for that reason. It is the filter every word that leaves TBG runs through.

Commercial-first positioning with selective residential. Twin Cities and central Minnesota. General contractors, property managers, community. A business that is local in the way the best local businesses are — known by name, referred by name, trusted with the big jobs because of the small ones.
signal_highlights: Project range $10K – $10M, unusual for a solo operator
Dual-register voice: faith inside, universal outside
Relationship moat: GCs, property managers, community
Matt is the signal — every client, every quote, every closeout

section_03_brand_we_built.headline: The foundation, set.
body: Before any of the systems work could begin, the brand had to hold. The goal was a system strong enough to support $15M of commercial construction without getting in its own way. Clean, substantial, quietly confident. Nothing trendy — commercial construction rewards timelessness.
Every deliverable below is complete and handed off.

deliverables.name: Logo system
deliverables.detail: Primary mark, icon, monochrome variants, favicon. Built for truck wraps, business cards, embroidered hats, and a 30-foot trailer wrap with equal ease.

deliverables.name: Color palette
deliverables.detail: Sovereign (#502A6F, deep purple), Restoration (#758B6C, sage-olive), Foundation (#F5F5F1, warm cream), Iron (#2B2B2B, near-black), Signal (#C9A34E, brass-gold accent). Every color carries meaning and earns its place.

deliverables.name: Typography system
deliverables.detail: Fraunces for display — editorial, substantial, with the right amount of character for a builder's brand. Inter Tight for body — modern, legible, unfussy. JetBrains Mono for technical surfaces and the Cornerstone UI.

deliverables.name: Brand guide PDF
deliverables.detail: Full usage rules for logo, color, type, spacing, voice, and application examples. The document a vendor, printer, or contractor can follow without guessing.

deliverables.name: Brand voice skill
deliverables.detail: Internal tooling that captures TBG's voice so every piece of copy — emails, proposals, social posts, quotes — runs through the same filter. Faith-grounded inside, universal outside.

deliverables.name: Mission, vision, tagline
deliverables.detail: Mission and vision developed through the embed. Tagline landed as: Your Project. Your Budget. Your Builder. Three beats. All three earned.

deliverables.name: Website — six pages
deliverables.detail: Written in the Breakthrough Advertising framework. Interactive wireframe approved, full build shipped. Pages cover: Home, Commercial, Residential, Process, About, Contact.

deliverables.name: Google My Business
deliverables.detail: Profile claimed, verified, optimized, and populated with Sage-produced assets. The first touchpoint for most local leads.

section_04_whats_being_built.eyebrow: Phase 2 · What's Being Built Right Now
section_04_whats_being_built.headline: The systems layer, in partnership with The G Fraction.
body: The brand is the skin. The systems are the skeleton. Phase 2 is where Geoffrey Makousky and The G Fraction take the brand foundation and architect the intelligence behind the business — the part that runs the pipeline, manages the jobs, and turns every finished project into the marketing for the next one.
This work is underway and the architecture is already documented in the Cornerstone Architecture Report (April 16, 2026). Short version below.

in_build.name: Cornerstone — Master Brain Thread
in_build.detail: The operational intelligence for TBG. Nine-card taxonomy covering identity, services, experience, pipeline, projects, relationships, operations, stewardship, and growth. Lives in one master thread; spawns child threads for deep work.

in_build.name: Base44 App Layer
in_build.detail: Matt's dashboard and the client-facing portal. CRM, project tracker, content generator, and prospect manager in one unified tool. Matt feeds it raw input — a sentence, a photo of notes, a scope sketch — and Cornerstone fills the gaps.

in_build.name: JSON Repository
in_build.detail: Voice rules, pricing structures, pipeline definitions, and agent configurations committed to a GitHub repo. External conscious memory so the brain stays lean.

in_build.name: GoHighLevel Sub-Account
in_build.detail: Pipeline mirror, campaign automations, and the CRM layer wired under Cornerstone.

in_build.name: Bland.ai + GHL Connection
in_build.detail: AI call handling for inbound leads, wired into the Cornerstone pipeline so no inquiry gets lost.

in_build.name: Supabase + pgvector Knowledge Base
in_build.detail: The retrieval layer behind Cornerstone — vector search over TBG's full operational knowledge so Matt can ask anything and get the right answer back.

in_build.name: End-to-End Automation Testing
in_build.detail: Full pipeline validation — prospect intake through project closeout — before Matt goes live.

in_build.name: Matt's Training & Handoff
in_build.detail: Working session where Matt learns his own system. From there on, he runs it.

section_05_revenue_and_growth.headline: From $500K solo to $15M on five crews, without losing the Matt signature.
body: The business case for Cornerstone isn't complicated: every lead that doesn't get followed up is a job that didn't get won, and every finished job that doesn't get documented is marketing you paid for and threw away. The system closes both gaps without asking Matt to work more hours.
Specifically:

growth_levers.title: Prospect capture at zero friction
growth_levers.detail: Matt drops a name, a photo, a scope sketch. Cornerstone captures, fills the gaps, builds the dossier, stages it for approval. Nothing is lost because nothing requires Matt to sit down at a keyboard at the end of a long day.

growth_levers.title: Dossier engine scales bidding
growth_levers.detail: A professional client-ready proposal gets generated in minutes, not hours. Matt can bid more jobs without more time — which is the single biggest constraint on solo operators.

growth_levers.title: Closeout automation as marketing
growth_levers.detail: Every finished project becomes a portfolio entry, a social post, a case study, a review request. Twenty minutes after the punch-list, the marketing is already done.

growth_levers.title: Prospecting engine (V2+)
growth_levers.detail: Outbound into property manager networks, GC relationships, and commercial property owners. Research, dossier, outreach — automated. This is how solo operator becomes the firm that gets called first.

growth_levers.title: Project Manager Agent (V2+)
growth_levers.detail: When job volume crosses the threshold, a dedicated thread per project takes over photo log, change orders, budget tracking, sub coordination. Matt goes from builder to operator.

year_5_2031.structure: 5 crew, full system, prospecting engine live

section_06_the_vision.headline: Taking Back Ground.
body: The name is not an accident. TBG is a builder's declaration — that craft, stewardship, and care can win back the ground the industry has conceded to bad work, bad communication, and bad faith.

What we are building, together, is a commercial construction company that scales without becoming what it set out to replace. A firm that can take a $6M ground-up and still answer the phone on a $15K punch-list. A brand that holds its faith-grounded core without making the external client feel like they're being preached to. A business Matt can hand his son someday without apology.

The systems Geoffrey is building do not replace the Matt signature. They protect it. They carry the weight of the admin, the documentation, the follow-up, the portfolio — so Matt can stay where he is most valuable: on the jobsite, on the call, in the room.

section_07_how_we_work.headline: Sage on the front. The G Fraction on the back. One team to you.
body: You work with two companies and you work with one system. Sage Media & Design holds the creative — brand, website, visual identity, voice, copy, and ongoing creative maintenance. The G Fraction holds the systems — Cornerstone brain, Base44 app, CRM, automations, AI call handling, and the infrastructure behind everything.

Everything you see as a finished artifact — a brand update, a new proposal template, a campaign, a portfolio post — passes through one of our hands before it reaches you. Nothing goes to a client without your sign-off. The system proposes. You approve.
principles: Parallel systems, not replacements. We never tear down what's working. We build beside it.
Voice preservation. The AI magnifies everything, including mistakes. The voice filter exists to protect yours.
Results over theory. We don't charge for cool builds. We charge for outcomes — jobs won, leads captured, revenue moved.
You own it. Brain, data, app, brand — all yours. Buyout clauses on every partnership.

section_08_next_step.headline: Your portal activates as Cornerstone goes live.
body: The dossier you are reading becomes page one of a shared workspace — the TBG portal. From there, it grows with us: brand plan, active projects, asset library, marketing, notes. You get a login and so does anyone on your team who needs one.
In the meantime, The G Fraction is building. Expect a working session with Geoffrey within the next two weeks to walk the Cornerstone architecture and start feeding in your current prospect queue.
If there is anything — a detail we got wrong, a client we missed, a concern about what's being built — drop it in the form below. We read everything.

projects.title: TBG Contracting — Full Brand Identity
projects.scope_notes: Logo system, color palette, typography, brand guide PDF, brand voice skill, mission/vision/tagline. Shipped with dual-register voice system and full application coverage from business card to 30-ft trailer wrap.

projects.title: TBG Contracting — Website Build
projects.scope_notes: Six pages, Breakthrough Advertising framework copy, interactive wireframe approved, full build shipped. Pages: Home, Commercial, Residential, Process, About, Contact.

projects.title: TBG Contracting — Google My Business
projects.scope_notes: Profile claimed, verified, optimized. Photos, categories, service areas, hours, description populated.

projects.title: TBG Contracting — Cornerstone Brain & Base44 App
projects.scope_notes: Master brain thread with 9-card taxonomy. Base44 app (CRM + PM + content generator unified). JSON repo (voice, pricing, pipeline, agents). GHL sub-account + pipeline + automations. Bland.ai + GHL connections. Supabase + pgvector KB. E2E automation testing. Matt training & handoff.

partners.name: The G Fraction
partners.notes: Sister company. Sage is the creative face; The G Fraction is the back-end engine. Full partnership brief: Sage Media Strategic Partnership (April 2026). On TBG specifically, The G Fraction leads Cornerstone brain architecture, Base44 app build, GHL/Bland integrations, and ongoing systems operations.

sage_g_fraction_partnership_brief.title: A Working Partnership — Sage Media + The G Fraction

voice_notes_for_app_consumers.voice_summary: Warm, direct, grounded. No hype. Pricing and intent always named explicitly. References craft and lived experience (kitchens, running a restaurant) when it earns its place. Prose leans short. Self-aware about tech-trap and agency-failure modes without lecturing.
do_not: Do not reference the Sage OS in the client-facing sections — the dossier speaks to Matt, not to operators.
Do not mechanize the prose. Keep the warmth.
do: Name the specific deliverable, not the abstract category.
Lead with observation, follow with proposal.
Close sections quietly. Let the page breathe.
